title: chionochloa rubra subsp. cuprea
intro: Chionochloa rubra subsp. cuprea, also known as Red Tussock or Copper Sedge, is a type of grass native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Chionochloa rubra subsp. cuprea include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, although it can also be made into a poultice or infused oil for topical use.
